topic: The government should ban single-use plastic bags

systemArguments.text: Plastic bags are bad for the environment
Animals get hurt by plastic bags
Other countries have banned them successfully
People will get used to bringing their own bags

goldenArguments.text: Society has a moral obligation to protect the environment for future generations
Government has a responsibility to prevent environmental harm when markets fail
Collective action is necessary to address environmental problems that individuals cannot solve alone
Sustainable practices should be prioritized over short-term convenience
Plastic bag bans reduce marine pollution and protect wildlife ecosystems
Single-use plastic bans decrease waste management costs for municipalities
Reusable bag adoption changes consumer behavior toward sustainability
Plastic bag bans have proven effective in reducing plastic waste in other jurisdictions

humanRationale: System arguments are overly simplistic and lack sophisticated reasoning. They state obvious environmental concerns but fail to articulate deeper principles about collective responsibility, government's role in market failures, or comprehensive policy analysis. Arguments read more like slogans than structured policy positions. Missing key philosophical foundations about environmental stewardship and the balance between regulation and individual freedom.
